purpose: PESS-style Dietary Exposure MCP demonstration for Endosulfan using public source anchors, governed EFSA OpenFoodTox reference values, and illustrative screening residue inputs.

sourceAnchors.title: Modeling external exposure to pesticides in human populations: Developing an exposure scenario generator
sourceAnchors.observedUse: PESS head-to-head comparison anchor for the dietary module.
lockedFacts: PESS demonstrates endosulfan, mancozeb, and glyphosate dietary exposure.
EFSA OpenFoodTox records an endosulfan ADI of 6.0 ug/kg bw/day (EFSA CONTAM, 2005) and an ARfD of 0.015 mg/kg bw/day (EFSA PPR, 2012).
EU MRLs for endosulfan are set at or near the analytical limit of quantification.

sourceAnchors.title: EFSA Chemical Hazards Database - OpenFoodTox
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Governed toxicological reference values (ADI/ARfD) used for health-reference context.
lockedFacts: OpenFoodTox record efsa.openfoodtox.endosulfan.adi.65 provides the ADI used here.
OpenFoodTox record efsa.openfoodtox.endosulfan.arfd.775 provides the ARfD.

sourceAnchors.title: EU legislation on pesticide maximum residue levels
sourceAnchors.observedUse: MRL-band context anchor for the illustrative screening residue inputs.
lockedFacts: Endosulfan is an organochlorine insecticide whose EU approval lapsed; EU MRLs sit at or near the analytical LOQ band (~0.01-0.05 mg/kg).

sourceAnchors.title: EU Pesticides Database
sourceAnchors.observedUse: Public source anchor for EU active-substance and MRL lookup context.

screeningInputPosture.residueValues: Illustrative regulatory-screening inputs within the observed EU MRL band; not row-level EFSA monitoring records.
screeningInputPosture.referenceValues: REAL governed EFSA OpenFoodTox values (ADI 0.006 mg/kg bw/day; ARfD 0.015 mg/kg bw/day).
screeningInputPosture.chemicalIdentityNote: DTXSID not locked in this public slice; preferredName + CASRN provided.

nonClaims: No EFSA PRIMo, DEEM, or PESS engine execution is claimed.
No official equivalence to EFSA monitoring datasets or dashboards is claimed.
No legal clearance, final regulatory decision, trade clearance, or PBPK internal-dose simulation is claimed.
